children.heading: Program established
children.label: a Program established
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior (hereafter “Secretary”), acting pursuant to the Reclamation Act of 1902 (Act of , ) and Acts amendatory thereof and supplementary thereto (hereafter “Federal reclamation laws”), is directed to undertake a program to investigate and identify opportunities for reclamation and reuse of municipal, industrial, domestic, and agricultural wastewater, and naturally impaired ground and surface waters, for the design and construction of demonstration and permanent facilities to reclaim and reuse wastewater, and to conduct research, including desalting, for the reclamation of wastewater and naturally impaired ground and surface waters.June 17, 190232 Stat. 388

children.heading: States included
children.label: b States included
children.text: Such program shall be limited to the States and areas referred to in section 1 of the Reclamation Act of 1902 (Act of , ) [] as amended, and the State of Hawaii.June 17, 190232 Stat. 38843 U.S.C. 391

children.heading: Agreements and regulations
children.label: c Agreements and regulations
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to enter into such agreements and promulgate such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purposes and provisions of sections 390h to 390h–39 of this title.

children.heading: San Luis Unit of Central Valley Project, California
children.label: d San Luis Unit of Central Valley Project, California
children.text: The Secretary shall not investigate, promote or implement, pursuant to sections 390h to 390h–39 of this title, any project intended to reclaim and reuse agricultural wastewater generated in the service area of the San Luis Unit of the Central Valley Project, California, except those measures recommended for action by the San Joaquin Valley Drainage Program in the report entitled A Management Plan for Agricultural Subsurface Drainage and Related Problems on the Westside San Joaquin Valley (September 1990).

children.heading: Authorization of new water recycling and reuse projects
children.label: e Authorization of new water recycling and reuse projects

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Non-Federal interests may submit proposals for projects eligible to be authorized pursuant to this section in the form of completed feasibility studies to the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: municipal, industrial, domestic, or agricultural wastewater; or

children.label: ii
children.text: impaired ground or surface waters.

children.heading: Guidelines
children.label: C Guidelines
children.text: Within 60 days of the enactment of this Act the Secretary shall issue guidelines for feasibility studies for water recycling and reuse projects to provide sufficient information for the formulation of the studies.

children.label: A
children.text: determining whether the study, and the process under which the study was developed, each comply with Federal laws and regulations applicable to feasibility studies of water recycling and reuse projects; and

children.label: B
children.text: the project is technically and financially feasible and provides a Federal benefit in accordance with the reclamation laws.

children.label: A
children.text: the results of the Secretary’s review of the study under paragraph (2), including a determination of whether the project is feasible;

children.label: B
children.text: any recommendations the Secretary may have concerning the plan or design of the project; and

children.label: C
children.text: any conditions the Secretary may require for construction of the project.

children.heading: Eligibility for funding
children.label: 4 Eligibility for funding
children.text: The non-Federal project sponsor of any project determined by the Secretary to be feasible under paragraph (3)(A) shall be eligible to apply to the Secretary for funding for the Federal share of the costs of planning, designing and constructing the project pursuant to subsection (f).

children.heading: Competitive grant program for the funding of water recycling and reuse projects
children.label: f Competitive grant program for the funding of water recycling and reuse projects

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a competitive grant program under which the non-Federal project sponsor of any project determined by the Secretary to be feasible under subsection (e)(3)(A) shall be eligible to apply for funding for the planning, design, and construction of the project, subject to subsection (g)(2).

children.label: A
children.text: has been identified by the United States Drought Monitor as experiencing severe, extreme, or exceptional drought at any time in the 4-year period before such funds are made available; or

children.label: B
children.text: was designated as a disaster area by a State during the 4-year period before such funds are made available.

children.label: A
children.text: Projects that are likely to provide a more reliable water supply for States and local governments.

children.label: B
children.text: Projects that are likely to increase the water management flexibility and reduce impacts on environmental resources from projects operated by Federal and State agencies.

children.label: C
children.text: Projects that are regional in nature.

children.label: D
children.text: Projects with multiple stakeholders.

children.label: E
children.text: Projects that provide multiple benefits, including water supply reliability, eco-system benefits, groundwater management and enhancements, and water quality improvements.
There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary of the Interior an additional $50,000,000 to remain available until expended.

Projects can only receive funding if enacted appropriations legislation designates funding to them by name, after the Secretary recommends specific projects for funding pursuant to subsection (f) and transmits such recommendations to the appropriate committees of Congress.
